title: micromeria lasiophylla subsp. palmensis
intro: Micromeria lasiophylla subsp. palmensis, also known as the Canary Islands thyme or Palmensis micromeria, is a subspecies of the Micromeria lasiophylla plant, a type of flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae. This plant is used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its essential oils and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Micromeria lasiophylla subsp. palmensis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and exhibit antimicrobial activity against certain pathogens. Herbal preparations of this plant can be used as teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
